title: rumex scutatus subsp. induratus
intro: Rumex scutatus subsp. induratus, also known as the narrow-leaved dock or bitter dock, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat or sorrel family (Polygonaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Rumex scutatus subsp. induratus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by boiling the leaves and roots in water or extracting the active compounds with a solvent like ethanol.
